summary: Partially decompiled and matched the previously empty `src/melee/mn/mnhyaku.c`, adding menu logic for the `mnHyaku` module, object creation, animation setup, SIS text handling, input-driven selection changes, and audio calls. The PR also exposed missing `lbAudioAx` prototypes needed by the new code and removed the lone blank line from empty `mnlanguage.c`. No PR body or review discussion was available, so intent beyond partial matching is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Added 273 lines to `src/melee/mn/mnhyaku.c`, replacing an effectively empty file with partial decompiled menu code.
Added static float data arrays `mnHyaku_803EF668`, `mnHyaku_803EF674`, and `mnHyaku_803EF680`, plus a static byte table `vals` used for SIS text IDs.
Added `StaticModelDesc mnHyaku_804A08E8` and references to external menu globals such as `mnHyaku_804D6C58`, `mn_804D6BB8`, `mn_804D6BC8`, and `mn_804A04F0`.
Created local placeholder structs for unclear data layouts, including `gobj_user_data`, an unnamed `mn_804D6BC8` extern struct, an unnamed `mn_804A04F0` extern struct, and `struct sislib_idk`.
Added two local inline helpers: one to request and replay JObj animation state based on a selected value, and one to refresh SIS text/user data associated with the menu object.

Added menu input handling in `mnHyaku_8024C68C`, including checks on `mn_80229624(4)`, audio feedback via `lbAudioAx_80024030`, wraparound selection changes from 0 through 5, and transitions through `mn_80229894`, `mn_802295AC`, `gm_801677E8`, and `mn_80229860`.

Added object setup in `mnHyaku_8024CB94`, including `GObj_Create`, `HSD_JObjLoadJoint`, `HSD_GObjObject_80390A70`, `GObj_SetupGXLink`, animation attachment, user-data allocation, assert-on-null behavior, and GObjProc installation.

Added scene/setup logic in `mnHyaku_8024CD64`, including audio initialization calls, archive section loading for `MenMainConKm_Top_*` resources, creation of the menu object, creation of an update proc, and a sound-effect play call.
Removed a single blank line from otherwise empty `src/melee/mn/mnlanguage.c`.
smart_moves: Kept uncertain symbols address-based instead of overnaming them, which preserves searchability and avoids false semantic claims during partial matching.
Used narrow local placeholder structs with explicit field offsets like `x0`, `x2`, `x4`, and `x8` to make progress without pretending the full menu/global layouts were known.
Factored repeated animation reset/replay behavior into a `static inline` helper, likely to reproduce compiler inlining while reducing duplicated source.
Factored repeated SIS text cleanup/recreation into a second `static inline`, again preserving likely inlining while keeping the main input logic readable.
Added only the missing `lbAudioAx` declarations needed by the matched code instead of broad header churn.

Used concrete archive section names, such as `MenMainConKm_Top_joint`, `MenMainConKm_Top_animjoint`, `MenMainConKm_Top_matanim_joint`, and `MenMainConKm_Top_shapeanim_joint`, which anchors the decompilation to observed asset names.
Preserved original assert behavior with `OSReport("Can't get user_data.\n")` and `__assert("mnhyaku.c", 344, "user_data")`, providing useful evidence for source file/line reconstruction.
decomp_lessons: For partially matched menu files, it is acceptable to introduce local placeholder structs when the fields needed for matching are known but the canonical type names or full layouts are not.
Inline helpers can be a practical matching tactic when the compiler appears to duplicate small routines, especially around animation state setup or object user-data updates.
GObj user data often needs a small custom struct plus `GObj_InitUserData` with `HSD_Free`; matching allocation size and initialization order is important.
Menu selection code may encode wraparound behavior directly with ternaries, as shown by selection index 0 wrapping to 5 and 5 wrapping to 0.
Audio header gaps commonly surface when decompiling menu code; add prototypes in shared headers once a caller requires them rather than leaving local declarations scattered.
Asset-section string names loaded through `lbArchive_LoadSections` are strong evidence for static model descriptors and should be retained exactly.
When global menu structs are not yet typed, using anonymous extern struct declarations with offset-like field names can unblock matching while flagging the need for later type cleanup.
naming_conventions: Matched/identified functions in this module use the `mnHyaku_` prefix plus address, e.g. `mnHyaku_8024C68C`, `mnHyaku_8024CB94`, and `mnHyaku_8024CD64`.
Unidentified local callbacks remain as `fn_` plus address, e.g. `fn_8024C9F0`, `fn_8024CA50`, and `fn_8024CAC8`.
Unknown globals retain module/address names such as `mnHyaku_804D6C58`, `mnHyaku_804A08E8`, `mn_804D6BB8`, `mn_804D6BC8`, and `mn_804A04F0`.
Unknown struct fields are named by offset-style placeholders such as `x0`, `x2`, `x4`, `x8`, `x10`, and `x11`.
Temporary or unclear local types are explicitly marked with provisional names like `gobj_user_data` and `struct sislib_idk`.
The added audio prototypes follow existing `lbAudioAx_` plus address naming conventions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `static inline` functions `mnHyaku_8024C68C_inline` and `mnHyaku_8024C68C_inline_2` to model repeated sequences while still allowing compiler inlining.
Preserved exact float constants such as `-0.1f`, `364.68332F`, `38.38772F`, and `0.0521F`, which are often sensitive for data and instruction matching.
Used static arrays for animation frame/value tables rather than embedding all constants inline, matching expected data layout for `mnHyaku_803EF668`, `mnHyaku_803EF674`, and `mnHyaku_803EF680`.
Used a macro `GET_GOBJ_USER_DATA(gobj)` to force the cast through `HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj)` in the SIS update helper.
Preserved call ordering for JObj animation setup: request animation, animate, restore previous frame, set flags/visibility via `mn_8022F3D8`, then animate again.
Modeled proc transitions explicitly with `HSD_GObjProc_8038FE24`, `HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54`, and assignment to `gobj_proc->flags_3`.
Used precise integer constants and unsigned suffixes in GObj and audio calls, such as `7U`, `0x80`, `0x7530`, `0x7FU`, and `0x40U`, which may matter for matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; observations are based only on the title, file list, and diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename or type `gobj_user_data`, `struct sislib_idk`, `mn_804D6BC8`, or `mn_804A04F0`.
Search subsequent `mnhyaku.c` work for whether `fn_8024C9F0`, `fn_8024CA50`, and `fn_8024CAC8` receive semantic names.
Check later audio header cleanup for canonical declarations of `lbAudioAx_80024030`, `lbAudioAx_80026F2C`, `lbAudioAx_8002702C`, `lbAudioAx_80027168`, and `lbAudioAx_80027648`.
Search for other menu modules using `MenMainConKm_Top_*` archive resources to consolidate model descriptor patterns.
Investigate whether the `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC` return type and field offsets around `idk[9]` and `idk[10]` were later formalized.
